content.title: 可以 (kěyǐ) – "Can/May"
content.body:
**可以** (kěyǐ) means "can," "may," or "is possible." It's commonly used to express availability or willingness:

- **我星期五可以。** (wǒ xīngqī wǔ kěyǐ.) – I can (do it) on Friday.
- **下午可以吗？** (xiàwǔ kěyǐ ma?) – Is afternoon okay?

content.title: Using 上 (shàng) for "Last"
content.body:
The word **上** (shàng) means "above" or "last/previous" when used with time words:

- **上个星期** (shàngge xīngqī) – last week
- **上个月** (shàngge yuè) – last month

Compare with 下 (xià, "next"):
- 上个月 (shàngge yuè) – last month
- 这个月 (zhège yuè) – this month  
- 下个月 (xiàge yuè) – next month

content.title: Answering "What Day Is It?"
content.body:
When someone asks **今天星期几？** (jīntiān xīngqī jǐ? – What day is today?), you respond with:

**今天星期** + [day number]

Example dialogue:
- A: 今天星期几？
- B: 今天星期二。 (jīntiān xīngqī èr.) – Today is Tuesday.

You can also ask about other days:
- 明天星期几？ (míngtiān xīngqī jǐ?) – What day is tomorrow?
- 昨天星期几？ (

content.title: 今年 (jīnnián) – "This Year"
content.body:
**今年** (jīnnián) means "this year." It combines 今 (jīn, "current") with 年 (nián, "year").

Example: 今年2024年。 (jīnnián èr líng èr sì nián.) – This year is 2024.

content.title: Time Word Patterns
content.body:
Chinese time words follow consistent patterns:

**Relative days:**
- 昨天 (zuótiān) – yesterday
- 今天 (jīntiān) – today
- 明天 (míngtiān) – tomorrow

**Relative years:**
- 去年 (qùnián) – last year
- 今年 (jīnnián) – this year
- 明年 (míngnián) – next year

**Relative weeks/months (with 个):**
- 上个星期 – last wee

content.title: 昨天 (zuótiān) – "Yesterday"
content.body:
**昨天** (zuótiān) means "yesterday." It combines 昨 (zuó, "previous") with 天 (tiān, "day").

Example: 昨天星期三。 (zuótiān xīngqī sān.) – Yesterday was Wednesday.

content.title: 月 (yuè) – "Month"
content.body:
**月** (yuè) means "month." Months are formed by number + 月:

- **一月** (yīyuè) – January
- **二月** (èryuè) – February
- **三月** (sānyuè) – March
- **四月** (sìyuè) – April
- **五月** (wǔyuè) – May
- **六月** (liùyuè) – June
- **七月** (qīyuè) – July
- **八月** (bāyuè) – August
- **九月** (jiǔyuè) – September
- **十

content.title: 号 (hào) – "Day of the Month"
content.body:
**号** (hào) means "day of the month" or "date." Days are formed by number + 号:

- **一号** (yī hào) – the 1st
- **十五号** (shíwǔ hào) – the 15th
- **二十三号** (èrshísān hào) – the 23rd

Example: 今天几号？ (jīntiān jǐ hào?) – What's the date today?

Note: **日** (rì, meaning "sun" or "day") is an alternative for

content.title: Talking About Birth Dates
content.body:
To ask "When is your birthday?" say:

**你的生日是几月几号？** (nǐ de shēngrì shì jǐ yuè jǐ hào?)

- 你的 (nǐ de) – your
- 生日 (shēngrì) – birthday
- 是 (shì) – is
- 几月几号 (jǐ yuè jǐ hào) – what month what day

Response: **我的生日是** + [month] + [day]

Example: 我的生日是五月十二号。 (wǒ de shēngrì shì wǔyuè shí'èr hào.) – My b

content.title: 这个星期 (zhège xīngqī) – "This Week"
content.body:
**这个星期** (zhège xīngqī) means "this week." It combines:
- 这个 (zhège) – "this"
- 星期 (xīngqī) – "week"

Example: 这个星期很忙。 (zhège xīngqī hěn máng.) – This week is very busy.

content.title: Date Format: Year-Month-Day
content.body:
Complete dates in Mandarin follow the pattern: **年 (year) + 月 (month) + 号 (day)**

Format: **X年X月X号**

Examples:
- **2024年3月15号** (èr líng èr sì nián sānyuè shíwǔ hào) – March 15, 2024
- **2023年12月1号** – December 1, 2023

This follows the logical order from largest to smallest time unit.

content.title: 明年 (míngnián) – "Next Year"
content.body:
**明年** (míngnián) means "next year." It combines 明 (míng, "bright/next") with 年 (nián, "year").

Example: 明年我去中国。 (míngnián wǒ qù Zhōngguó.) – Next year I'm going to China.

content.title: 明天 (míngtiān) – "Tomorrow"
content.body:
**明天** (míngtiān) means "tomorrow." It combines 明 (míng, "bright/next") with 天 (tiān, "day").

Example: 明天星期六。 (míngtiān xīngqī liù.) – Tomorrow is Saturday.

content.title: 年 (nián) – "Year"
content.body:
**年** (nián) means "year." To form a year, say each digit individually followed by 年.

Examples:
- **2024年** – èr líng èr sì nián (2-0-2-4 year)
- **2019年** – èr líng yī jiǔ nián (2-0-1-9 year)

Note: Use 零 (líng) for "zero" in years.

content.title: 星期 (xīngqī) – "Week"
content.body:
The word **星期** (xīngqī) means "week" and is used to form days of the week. It literally means "star period."

When asking "What day is it?" you say: **星期几？** (xīngqī jǐ?) using the question word 几 (jǐ) for "which number."

content.title: 下个月 (xiàge yuè) – "Next Month"
content.body:
**下个月** (xiàge yuè) means "next month." It combines:
- 下个 (xiàge) – "next"
- 月 (yuè) – "month"

You can also use 下 (xià, "next/below") with other time words:
- 下个星期 (xiàge xīngqī) – next week
- 下午 (xiàwǔ) – afternoon (literally "below noon")

content.title: Days of the Week: Saturday and Sunday
content.body:
The weekend days are:

- **星期六** (xīngqī liù) – Saturday ("week six")
- **星期日** (xīngqī rì) or **星期天** (xīngqī tiān) – Sunday

Sunday has two common forms: 日 (rì, "sun/day") and 天 (tiān, "day/sky"). Both are equally correct and widely used.

content.title: Asking About Dates
content.body:
To ask "What's the date?" you can say:

- **今天几月几号？** (jīntiān jǐ yuè jǐ hào?) – What month and day is today?
- **今天几号？** (jīntiān jǐ hào?) – What day (of the month) is today?

Response format: **今天** + [month] + [day]

Example:
- Q: 今天几月几号？
- A: 今天三月二十五号。 (jīntiān sānyuè èrshíwǔ hào.) – Today is Ma

content.title: 今天 (jīntiān) – "Today"
content.body:
**今天** (jīntiān) means "today." It combines 今 (jīn, "current/now") with 天 (tiān, "day").

Examples:
- 今天星期几？ (jīntiān xīngqī jǐ?) – What day is today?
- 今天星期一。 (jīntiān xīngqī yī.) – Today is Monday.

content.title: Complete Date Expressions
content.body:
Full dates can include the day of the week:

**[Year]年[Month]月[Day]号星期[Day of Week]**

Example: **2024年3月15号星期五** (èr líng èr sì nián sānyuè shíwǔ hào xīngqī wǔ) – Friday, March 15, 2024

However, more commonly you'll see:
- Just the date: 3月15号
- Date + day: 3月15号星期五
- Or in conversation, just the

content.title: Planning a Meeting
content.body:
**Dialogue:**

李明：我们什么时候见面？  
(Lǐ Míng: wǒmen shénme shíhou jiànmiàn?)

王丽：下个星期怎么样？  
(Wáng Lì: xiàge xīngqī zěnmeyàng?)

李明：好。星期几？  
(Lǐ Míng: hǎo. xīngqī jǐ?)

王丽：星期三，五月十号。  
(Wáng Lì: xīngqī sān, wǔyuè shí hào.)

**Vocabulary:**
- 我们 (wǒmen) – we/us
- **什么时候** (shénme shíhou) – when/what time. Us

content.title: Days of the Week: Monday to Friday
content.body:
Days of the week follow a numbered pattern using 星期 + number:

- **星期一** (xīngqī yī) – Monday ("week one")
- **星期二** (xīngqī èr) – Tuesday ("week two")
- **星期三** (xīngqī sān) – Wednesday ("week three")
- **星期四** (xīngqī sì) – Thursday ("week four")
- **星期五** (xīngqī wǔ) – Friday ("week five")

Note

content.title: 去年 (qùnián) – "Last Year"
content.body:
**去年** (qùnián) means "last year." It combines 去 (qù, "go/past") with 年 (nián, "year").

Example: 去年我在北京。 (qùnián wǒ zài Běijīng.) – Last year I was in Beijing.
